title: rupicapnos africana subsp. graciliflora
intro: Rupicapnos africana subsp. graciliflora, also known as the African burn plant or burn plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rupicapnos africana subsp. graciliflora include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from pain and itching. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make topical creams, ointments, and salves, as well as teas and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties.
